text:
Department of Neurology 

Clinic Date: 23 April 2014 

Dr Amelia Kerr  
St George’s University Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust  
Department of Neurology, Blackshaw Road, London SW17 0QT  

Dear Dr Amelia Kerr,  

Michael Turner, DOB: 14-02-1987, Hospital No: H392774 NHS No. 9031142286  
27 Brookfield Close, Mitcham, CR4 1QX 

Diagnoses: 

Recurrent unprovoked seizures under investigation; prior normal brain MRI (February 2014). Ambulatory EEG (March 2014) without clear lateralising features. 

Current Medication: 

Levetiracetam 1,000 mg twice daily  
Clobazam 10 mg at night (as adjunct, started January 2014) 

Plan: 

- Continue current antiepileptic regimen without change  
- Maintain reduced alcohol intake and adherence to regular sleep schedule  
- Arrange nurse-led telephone review in 3 months; face-to-face review in 12 months or sooner if breakthrough events occur  
- Seizure diary to be continued; safety advice reinforced (bathing supervision, cooking precautions) 

I reviewed Michael Turner in clinic today. Since the medication adjustment in January and a concerted effort to limit alcohol exposure and other potential triggers, there has been a clear trend toward improvement. Seizure burden has been substantially reduced following dose adjustment, with only six focal impaired-awareness seizures reported so far this year. These events have been shorter, with faster recovery and no generalised convulsions. There have been no injuries, and no emergency attendances were required. 

Michael Turner reports better sleep regularity and has reduced weekend alcohol intake, which they feel has helped. No new neurological symptoms were reported. Physical and neurological examination today was unremarkable. Routine bloods (U&E, LFTs) in March were within reference ranges. 

Given the ongoing improvement and good tolerability, I have recommended continuing the present regimen and maintaining the current lifestyle modifications. We discussed driving regulations and Michael Turner confirms they are not currently driving. Written safety information was provided. 

I will arrange a nurse-led telephone review in three months to monitor progress and will see Michael Turner in clinic in one year, or earlier if seizure frequency increases or new features emerge. 

With best wishes,

entities.text: only six focal impaired-awareness seizures reported so far this year
Seizure burden has been substantially reduced following dose adjustment, with only six focal impaired-awareness seizures reported so far this year

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter states: "only six focal impaired-awareness seizures reported so far this year." Clinic date is 23 April 2014, which is in the fourth month of the year. "So far this year" implies from 1 January 2014 up to 23 April 2014, approximately 4 months. Therefore, the current seizure frequency can be normalized as 6 per 4 months.
